reasoning: Historically, the WHO has declared global pandemics roughly 4 to 5 times over the last century (e.g., 1918, 1957, 1968, 2009, 2020), which translates to an empirical annual base rate of ~4-5%. While the continuing spread of H5N1 avian influenza in US dairy cattle represents an elevated zoonotic risk, sustained human-to-human transmission has not occurred and mutation into a pandemic strain in any single given year is unlikely. Furthermore, the WHO is notoriously conservative in formally using the term 'pandemic', often preferring 'Public Health Emergency of International Concern' (PHEIC) for significant but less explosive outbreaks (e.g., mpox, Ebola, Zika). Thus, the probability of a formal pandemic declaration in 2025 stays consistent with the historical baseline.
